stories.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Iran Conflict Energy Pressures
stories.summary: The Fed kept interest rates unchanged as persistent inflation driven by surging energy prices from the US-Iran conflict complicated monetary policy. Gulf oil flow disruptions have pushed national gas averages to $3.90 per gallon, forcing Chair Powell to balance economic slowdown risks against inflation control.

stories.headline: Trump Criticizes NATO Allies Over Lack of Support in Iran Campaign
stories.summary: President Trump blasted NATO members for not assisting US strikes on Iranian targets as Tehran retaliated by hitting Gulf energy infrastructure. The criticism stems from Trump's unilateral push to secure the Strait of Hormuz without allied backing, escalating tensions on day 19 of the conflict.

stories.headline: Counterterrorism Director Resigns Over Iran Policy Disagreements
stories.summary: The counterterrorism director resigned amid internal Trump administration splits on Iran war escalation, following US bunker-buster strikes that killed senior Iranian figures. The resignation highlights deepening divisions as intelligence officials face Congressional grilling over war risks and lack of pre-briefing on retaliatory threats.

stories.headline: Partial Government Shutdown Causes Airport Chaos as TSA Workers Go Unpaid
stories.summary: Nearly 10% of TSA agents called out sick without pay during a shutdown linked to Iran war funding debates, causing massive airport disruptions. The situation compounds economic strain as federal operations falter amid heightened security concerns including an IED incident at MacDill Air Force Base.

stories.headline: Judge Challenges Musk and DOGE Over USAID Closure
stories.summary: A federal judge is scrutinizing Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency for shutting down USAID as part of broader Trump-era cuts to federal agencies. The legal challenge questions the enforcement authority behind DOGE's agency closures and their impact on foreign aid tied to domestic budget priorities.

stories.headline: Gas Prices Hit $3.90 Average as Iran Strikes Disrupt Gulf Energy Supply
stories.summary: Iran's barrages on Gulf infrastructure and US-Israeli responses targeting Iranian gas fields have crippled oil supply through key shipping straits. The supply disruption is driving up US fuel costs and broader market volatility, worsening the inflation outlook even as Trump prioritizes restoring flows.

stories.headline: UK Seeks US Cooperation in Expanding Epstein Investigation
stories.summary: British authorities investigating Jeffrey Epstein's network are formally requesting US assistance, focusing on cross-border ties amid ongoing revelations. The probe potentially intersects with regulatory scrutiny on powerful figures during a period of heightened economic and geopolitical turmoil.

stories.headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 'Thinking' Model with Million-Token Context
stories.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 with enhanced step-by-step reasoning, an 'extreme thinking' mode for complex problems, and million-token context windows. The model reflects the industry shift toward deliberative inference over raw scale, enabling enterprise workflows like software interaction and large-scale data processing.

stories.headline: DeepSeek Launches V4 with 1 Trillion Parameters and Multimodal Capabilities
stories.summary: DeepSeek V4 introduces MODEL1 architecture with tiered KV cache achieving 40% memory reduction and Sparse FP8 decoding for 1.8x inference speedup. The release consolidates text, image, audio, and video into a single model, simplifying developer integrations while prioritizing knowledge density over parameter count.

stories.headline: Google DeepMind Unveils Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite and Deep Think for Math Breakthroughs
stories.summary: Flash-Lite targets cost-efficient developer workloads while Deep Think autonomously solved open math problems including Erdős Conjectures, scoring 90% on IMO-ProofBench. Google's Bayesian teaching method enables LLMs to dynamically update beliefs on new evidence, advancing agentic AI capabilities.

stories.headline: Cerebras Partners with AWS for Fastest AI Inference via Bedrock
stories.summary: AWS deployed Cerebras CS-3 systems with disaggregated architecture using Trainium for prefill and WSE for decode, boosting token throughput 5x. The partnership enables high-speed enterprise inference on open-source LLMs and Nova models through AWS Bedrock, significantly reducing inference costs.

stories.headline: Ai2 Releases Olmo Hybrid Achieving 2x Data Efficiency
stories.summary: The Allen Institute's 7B-parameter open model combines transformer attention and linear recurrent layers, matching Olmo 3 accuracy on MMLU with 49% fewer training tokens. The hybrid architecture proves that combining attention mechanisms can rival pure transformers while dramatically lowering training barriers for enterprises.

stories.headline: Industry Trend: Diffusion Models and Efficiency Gains Reshape AI Landscape
stories.summary: March 2026 sees growing focus on knowledge density in pre-training, native multimodality across leading models, and emerging text diffusion for parallel token generation. These developments are cutting inference costs and enabling more capable agentic enterprise tools, with predictions of mainstream diffusion LLMs by year-end.

stories.headline: AI Startups Dominate Q1 2026 Venture Capital with Mega-Rounds
stories.summary: AI companies continue to capture the lion's share of venture funding with multiple mega-rounds exceeding $500M in Q1 2026. The concentration reflects investor conviction that AI infrastructure and applications represent generational opportunities, with robotics and cybersecurity also attracting outsized capital.

stories.headline: SparkLabs Launches $20M Fund with King Saud University
stories.summary: SparkLabs announced a $20 million fund partnered with King Saud University, signaling increased VC attention to innovation ecosystems outside Silicon Valley. The fund aims to foster opportunities for engineers and entrepreneurs in Saudi Arabia by combining academic talent with global investors.

stories.headline: Emerging Tech Startups Attract Record $33.1B in Early-Stage Capital
stories.summary: Venture firms deployed $33.1 billion across 874 pre-seed, seed, and early-stage deals in emerging technologies, setting a new record despite lower overall deal volume. ETI-backed startups demonstrated superior performance with a 9.2% exit rate and 4.0% unicorn rate compared to broader venture averages.

stories.headline: Capital Super-Concentration Creates Two-Tier Funding Market
stories.summary: While AI, fintech, robotics, and cybersecurity startups secure large rounds rapidly, traditional SaaS and software companies face stricter financing conditions and valuation pressure. This bifurcation reflects a shift toward high-conviction bets in transformative technologies rather than broad-based startup funding.

stories.headline: Fintech and Healthtech See Renewed Institutional Interest
stories.summary: Fintech attracted $2.1 billion and healthtech/wellness matched at $2.1 billion in recent venture activity, while climate tech drew $1.9 billion. Biotechnology staged a notable rebound with $657 million across 15 deals, many involving clinical-stage assets and AI-driven drug discovery.

stories.headline: Growth-Stage Funding Remains Constrained While Seed Stays Resilient
stories.summary: Seed-stage investing continues with strong deal flow in AI and automation, but growth-stage capital remains tightened with investors demanding stronger fundamentals and cash flow visibility. Series B and C rounds are expected to proceed more slowly with greater discipline throughout 2026.

stories.headline: EIA Forecasts Record 86GW US Power Capacity Additions in 2026
stories.summary: The US Energy Information Administration predicts developers will add a record 86GW of new utility-scale power capacity in 2026, with solar PV dominating at 43.4GW (51% of total), up 60% from 2025. Texas will host 40% of new solar including the 837MW Tehuacana Creek 1 project co-located with 418MW storage.

stories.headline: SEIA Projects US Battery Storage Deployments to Surge to 70GWh in 2026
stories.summary: The Solar Energy Industries Association forecasts US battery storage deployments will reach 70GWh in 2026, following 57GWh added in 2025. Over half of utility-scale storage will be paired with solar in southwestern states, underscoring storage's growing role in grid reliability.

stories.headline: California Orders Utilities to Procure 6GW Non-Fossil Capacity by 2032
stories.summary: The California Public Utilities Commission mandated load-serving entities to add 6GW of zero-emitting capacity from 2029-2032 to address reliability shortfalls from demand growth. The order targets grid stability amid rising electrification needs across the state.

stories.headline: US Wind Capacity Rebounds with 11.8GW Additions Including Major Offshore Projects
stories.summary: Wind additions are set to double to 11.8GW in 2026, led by New Mexico, Texas, Illinois, and Wyoming comprising nearly 60% of total. Key projects include the 3,650MW SunZia onshore wind farm and delayed offshore projects Vineyard Wind 1 (800MW) and Revolution Wind (715MW).

stories.headline: Deloitte Warns of Slower Renewables Growth Post-2026 Amid FEOC Rules
stories.summary: Deloitte projects US solar, wind, and storage additions could drop to 30-66GW annually from 2026-2030 due to new foreign entity of concern sourcing restrictions on China-linked supply chains. Despite a potential 2026 surge from safe-harbor projects, longer-term growth faces structural headwinds.

stories.headline: Iran Conflict Disrupts Gulf Oil Flows, Spiking Global Energy Prices
stories.summary: Iranian strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure and US-Israeli responses targeting Iranian gas fields have disrupted oil supply through key shipping straits. The disruption has pushed US gas averages to $3.90/gallon and created broader market volatility across global energy commodities.

stories.headline: Astellas and Vir Biotechnology Partner on Prostate Cancer T-Cell Engager
stories.summary: Astellas Pharma and Vir Biotechnology announced a global collaboration to co-develop VIR-5500, a dual-masked CD3 T-cell engager targeting PSMA for prostate cancer treatment. Vir is eligible for up to $1.37 billion in milestones plus tiered royalties, with updated Phase 1 data to be presented at the 2026 ASCO GU Symposium.

stories.headline: Fierce Healthcare Names 'Fierce 15 of 2026' Top Innovators
stories.summary: Fierce Healthcare published its annual spotlight on leading companies in healthcare providers, payers, and technology innovation. The report highlights key industry developments including economic strains in emergency medicine and efforts to reimagine prior authorization for greater transparency.

stories.headline: Moody's Issues Negative 2026 Outlook for Health Insurers
stories.summary: Moody's forecasted a negative outlook for health insurers in 2026, citing intensifying cost pressures amid broader healthcare economics challenges. The warning aligns with discussions on emergency department financial strains and lawmakers considering curbs on provider consolidation to control costs.

stories.headline: Stemtech Launches 'Revolution 2026' Cellular Wellness Initiative
stories.summary: Stemtech announced a major initiative marking a new era in proactive, science-backed cellular-level wellness after 20 years in stem cell nutrition. The multi-channel growth model emphasizes natural plant-based ingredients and expansion into oral, skin care, and pet health products targeting anti-aging demand.

stories.headline: HLTH USA Conference Draws 25,000 to Focus on AI and Data in Healthcare
stories.summary: The HLTH USA event gathered 25,000 professionals from pharma, tech, AI, and health systems in Las Vegas to discuss ROI, data workflows, and transformative healthcare innovations. The conference reflects growing convergence between AI capabilities and healthcare delivery optimization.

stories.headline: Scientists Reveal Cancer Risk of Popular Anti-Aging Compound
stories.summary: Researchers discovered that a molecule linked to longevity may have dual effects—supporting healthy cells while potentially accelerating cancer growth. The team identified eIF5A2 as a promising therapeutic target that could selectively slow cancer progression without interfering with beneficial longevity effects of related compounds.

stories.headline: Eric Verdin Projects Anti-Aging Drugs by 2030
stories.summary: Leading aging researcher Eric Verdin discussed the trajectory of anti-aging drug development, projecting that by 2030 the field will have drugs demonstrably targeting the aging process. The discussion covered emerging interventions including CD38 inhibitors that extend lifespan in mice and the role of creatine in supporting mitochondrial health.

stories.headline: Longevity Market Expected to Grow at 6% CAGR from $15.8B Base
stories.summary: The longevity and anti-senescence therapy market is projected to grow from $15.8 billion in 2025 at a 6% compound annual growth rate, driven by expanding clinical programs and growing older populations. Increased public and private funding for aging biology research is accelerating commercialization timelines.

stories.headline: Life Biosciences Begins Human Trials for Epigenetic Reprogramming Therapy
stories.summary: Life Biosciences has entered human trials for its partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy, marking a significant milestone for the longevity biotech sector. Other notable developments include Junevity showing preclinical success in diabetes and obesity treatment using age-reversal approaches.

stories.headline: SpaceX Launches 25 Starlink Satellites from Vandenberg on 25th Mission of 2026
stories.summary: SpaceX successfully deployed 25 Starlink V2 Mini satellites on the Starlink 17-31 mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base, marking the company's 25th Starlink mission of 2026. The Falcon 9 booster landed successfully, supporting continued expansion of the low Earth orbit broadband constellation.

stories.headline: NASA Astronauts Begin Spacewalks to Prepare ISS for New Solar Arrays
stories.summary: NASA astronauts conducted spacewalks 94 and 95 outside the International Space Station on March 18 to prepare for installing two roll-out solar arrays. The extravehicular activities aim to enhance the station's power capabilities amid ongoing operations and growing energy demands.

stories.headline: NASA Shares Artemis II Flight Readiness Review Update
stories.summary: NASA hosted a news conference to discuss progress on the Artemis II crewed lunar mission, with the Administrator providing updates on timeline and readiness. The mission represents a critical step toward returning humans to the Moon as part of the broader Artemis program.

stories.headline: SpaceX Delivers EchoStar 25 to Geosynchronous Orbit
stories.summary: SpaceX achieved its first 2026 launch to geosynchronous transfer orbit with the EchoStar 25 communications satellite from Cape Canaveral. The deployment supports commercial satellite services and demonstrates Falcon 9's continued versatility across different orbital regimes.

stories.headline: JAXA HTV-X1 Cargo Spacecraft Departs ISS After Delivering 12,000 Pounds of Supplies
stories.summary: JAXA's uncrewed HTV-X1 spacecraft departed the ISS on March 6 after delivering 12,000 pounds of supplies and experiments. The logistics mission underscores ongoing international collaboration for station resupply and scientific research aboard the orbital laboratory.

stories.headline: SpaceX Crew-12 Astronauts Continue Science Expedition on ISS
stories.summary: Four astronauts launched on NASA's SpaceX Crew-12 mission in late February are conducting ongoing experiments aboard the International Space Station. The crew supports a wide range of scientific investigations as part of the station's continuous research program.

stories.headline: 2026 NFL Draft Rankings: Top 50 Prospects Released
stories.summary: ESPN's Field Yates published his top 50 prospects for the upcoming NFL draft, providing early evaluation of college players preparing for the professional level. The rankings reflect shifting team needs and emerging talent as franchises prepare for draft day selections.

stories.headline: Miami (OH) Upsets SMU in NCAA Tournament with Record Three-Point Shooting
stories.summary: Miami of Ohio defeated SMU in a First Four matchup by sinking 16 three-pointers, securing their first NCAA tournament win since 1999. The upset marks a significant milestone for the program and continues March Madness tradition of bracket-busting performances.

stories.headline: Shohei Ohtani Reaches 5th Inning in Spring Training Progression
stories.summary: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani continued building toward regular season readiness by reaching the fifth inning in his latest spring training appearance. The development indicates the two-way phenom is on track for full pitching duties as Opening Day approaches.

stories.headline: 'Meal Ticket' McDonald's All-American Documentary Premieres on Prime Video
stories.summary: A new documentary chronicling the 49-year history of the McDonald's All-American Games premiered on Amazon Prime Video. The film provides an in-depth look at one of basketball's most prestigious amateur showcases and its impact on launching NBA careers.

stories.headline: Seattle Kraken Expands Regional Reach with TEGNA and Amazon Distribution Deals
stories.summary: The Seattle Kraken is establishing itself in the Pacific Northwest market through new distribution agreements with TEGNA and Amazon. The strategic media partnerships are designed to expand the NHL franchise's regional viewership and strengthen its market position.

stories.headline: MLS 2026 Season Launches with New Primary Ticketing Partners
stories.summary: Multiple new primary ticketing platforms began operations with the start of the 2026 Major League Soccer season. The changes reflect evolving approaches to ticket distribution, fan access, and revenue optimization across the league's expanding franchise base.
